metadata.description: NPC archetype guidance for LLM-driven NPC creation
metadata.note: These are seeds, not chains. LLM may override, skip, or add tags.

village_blacksmith.description: A master craftsperson in a small settlement
village_blacksmith.narrative_seed: Skilled at their craft, known locally, tied to the town through generations or circumstance. Protective of their craft's reputation, wary of trends that bypass traditional methods.
village_blacksmith.affinity_notes: Smiths typically value nobles (they commission weapons) and distrust merchants (who undercut prices). Affinity modulated by local sentiment.

merchant_trader.description: A traveling or settled merchant working for a larger guild
merchant_trader.narrative_seed: Mobile, profit-driven, networked across regions. Sees opportunity in supply chains and market gaps. May be cynical about local politics.

suggested_tags.rationale: They trade here but aren't rooted

merchant_trader.affinity_notes: Merchants have affinity toward wealthy NPCs and cities. May be suspicious of isolationist factions. Affinity can shift rapidly based on profit.

town_guard.description: A soldier or watchman defending a settlement
town_guard.narrative_seed: Duty-bound, protective, often concerned with local threats and order. May be cynical or idealistic depending on tenure. Reports to authority.
town_guard.affinity_notes: High affinity for order and law. Affinity varies by nation's current politics. May distrust merchants or separatists depending on assignment.

village_mayor.description: A local administrator and community leader
village_mayor.narrative_seed: Responsible for the settlement's welfare. Balances local needs against external pressures. Often carries burden of difficult decisions.

suggested_tags.rationale: Central to their identity and responsibility
May have been granted or earned title

village_mayor.affinity_notes: Deep ties to locality. Affinity depends on whether they see nation as protective or exploitative. May have complex relationship with merchants (trade vs. fair dealing).

fisher.description: A person who makes their living from water
fisher.narrative_seed: Independent, knowledgeable about water and seasons. May live on margins of society. Connected to guild or solo operator.
fisher.affinity_notes: Fishers often value independence and nature. Affinity toward merchants depends on whether they feel exploited. May have ties to coast-specific factions.

scholar.description: A learned person devoted to knowledge
scholar.narrative_seed: Values learning, research, and truth. May be idealistic or jaded about knowledge's impact on the world. Often isolated from common folk.

suggested_tags.rationale: Often patronized or trained by nobility

scholar.affinity_notes: Scholars may have complex relationships with power. Affinity depends on whether they're independent or institutional. May value certain ideologies highly.

wanderer.narrative_seed: Rootless, independent, experienced across regions. May be escaping something or seeking something. Sees different perspectives.
wanderer.affinity_notes: Wanderers often have baseline affinities across nations (from experience). May be cynical about factionalism. Quick to move if local sentiment turns.

guidelines.use_in_prompt: If NPC role matches an archetype, include suggested_tags. LLM may override, combine, or ignore.
guidelines.override_note: Archetypes are seeds, not constraints. LLM should feel free to create unique tags or personalities.
guidelines.location_templating: Replace {location_name} and {nation_name} with actual geography from geographic-map.json
guidelines.affinity_note: Affinity is NOT stored in archetype. LLM infers from narrative and context. Affinity overrides are applied ONLY if NPC diverges significantly from location defaults.
